 {"id":"wa-rnf.1","title":"Shell completions: static clap derivation + dynamic pane/workflow completion","description":"
# Shell Completions

## Purpose
Enable tab completion for all wa commands, including dynamic values like pane IDs and workflow names.

## Static Completions (clap derive)
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(name = "wa")]
struct Cli {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    command: Commands,
}

// In main.rs
if let Some(shell) = args.completions {
    clap_complete::generate(shell, &mut cli, "wa", &mut io::stdout());
}
```

## Dynamic Completions
For pane IDs:
```bash
$ wa send --pane <TAB>
1 (codex @ /home/user/project)
3 (claude @ /home/user/other)
```

For workflow names:
```bash
$ wa workflow run <TAB>
handle_compaction    handle_usage_limits
```

## Implementation
Use clap_complete's custom completer:
```rust
fn complete_pane_id(current: &str) -> Vec<CompletionCandidate> {
    // Query running wa instance for pane list
    // Format as completion candidates
}
```

## Installation
```bash
# Bash
wa completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/wa

# Zsh
wa completions zsh > ~/.zfunc/_wa

# Fish
wa completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/wa.fish
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static completions for all commands
- [ ] Dynamic completion for pane IDs
- [ ] Dynamic completion for workflow names
- [ ] Installation instructions in setup wizard
- [ ] Works in bash, zsh, fish

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

 {"id":"wa-rnf.2","title":"Smart command aliases: built-in short forms + user-configurable aliases","description":"
# Smart Command Aliases

## Purpose
Provide muscle-memory-friendly shortcuts for common operations.

## Built-in Aliases
```
was  → wa status
wae  → wa events --unhandled
waq  → wa query
waw  → wa watch
wat  → wa timeline
wad  → wa doctor
```

## Implementation
In wa.toml:
```toml
[aliases]
s = "status"
e = "events --unhandled"
q = "query"
t = "timeline"
d = "doctor"
```

In code:
```rust
fn resolve_alias(args: &[String], config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {
    if let Some(alias) = config.aliases.get(&args[0]) {
        let expanded = shell_words::split(alias)?;
        [expanded, args[1..].to_vec()].concat()
    } else {
        args.to_vec()
    }
}
```

## Shell Aliases (Optional)
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
alias was='wa status'
alias wae='wa events --unhandled'
```

Generated by: `wa setup aliases bash >> ~/.bashrc`

## Listing Aliases
```
$ wa aliases
Built-in:
  s  → status
  e  → events --unhandled
  ...

User-defined:
  x  → export --format=jsonl
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Built-in aliases work
- [ ] User aliases configurable in wa.toml
- [ ] `wa aliases` lists all aliases
- [ ] Shell alias generation helper
- [ ] No conflicts with subcommand names

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

 {"id":"wa-rnf.3","title":"Progressive disclosure: default/verbose/debug output levels","description":"# Progressive disclosure (verbosity)

## Goal
Show essential information by default, with increasing detail available on request, without sacrificing scriptability.

## Key design rule
- `-v/-vv` controls **how much** information we include.
- `--format {auto|plain|json}` controls **how** we render it.

This keeps CLI behavior consistent and avoids a proliferation of ad-hoc `--json` flags.

## Verbosity levels
- Default (no flag): Essential info only
- `-v`: Additional context
- `-vv`: Debug details

## JSON mode
- Use `--format json` for machine-readable output.
- JSON output must be schema-stable and compatible with non-TTY usage.

## Example: `wa status`
```bash
# Default
$ wa status
Panes: 4 observed, 1 ignored
Events: 2 unhandled

# Verbose
$ wa status -v
Panes:
  1: codex [PromptActive] /home/user/project (last: 2m ago)
  3: claude [CommandRunning] /home/user/other (last: 5s ago)
  5: local [Ignored] (exclude rule: title=htop)
  7: gemini [PromptActive] /home/user/gemini (last: 30s ago)

Events (unhandled):
  - codex.usage_limit_reached (Pane 1, 2m ago)
  - session.compaction (Pane 3, 5m ago)

# Debug
$ wa status -vv
[as above, plus:]
Health:
  Queue depth: 0
  Ingest lag: 12ms
  DB size: 1.2GB
  Circuit breakers: all closed

# Machine
$ wa status --format json | jq .
```

## Implementation sketch
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
struct Cli {
    #[arg(short, long, action = ArgAction::Count)]
    verbose: u8,
}

fn format_status(status: &Status, verbosity: u8) -> String {
    match verbosity {
        0 => format_status_brief(status),
        1 => format_status_verbose(status),
        _ => format_status_debug(status),
    }
}
```

## Consistency rules
- All human commands support `-v` and `-vv`.
- Default shows what the user needs to know.
- Verbose adds context for understanding.
- Debug adds internal state for troubleshooting.
- `--format json` output must not contain ANSI escapes and must be schema-stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands support `-v/-vv`.
- [ ] Default output is clean and minimal.
- [ ] Verbose adds useful context.
- [ ] Debug shows internal state.
- [ ] Consistent pattern across all commands.

## Testing
- Unit tests for verbosity routing (brief/verbose/debug paths).
- Contract tests:
  - `--format json` schema validation
  - no ANSI escapes in non-TTY/plain/json
- Help/output snapshot tests where appropriate (normalized).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMeadow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:23.666085891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T04:53:54.213508086Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T04:53:54.213378616Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.3","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":101,"issue_id":"wa-rnf.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progressive disclosure implemented: changed Cli.verbose from bool to u8 with ArgAction::Count (-v, -vv), updated RenderContext.verbose from bool to u8, added is_verbose()/is_debug() convenience methods. Updated all command handlers, sub-command structs (RobotCommands, RulesCommands), and function signatures. Added 8 unit tests for verbosity routing (level 0/1/2/3, builder chain, detail rendering). 1054 wa-core lib tests pass. All 15 E2E stop tests pass. cargo check clean.","created_at":"2026-01-29T04:53:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-rnf.4","title":"Consistent help patterns: examples, cross-references, unified style","description":"
# Consistent Help Patterns

## Purpose
Make help text comprehensive, consistent, and genuinely helpful.

## Help Structure
Every command has:
1. One-line description
2. Usage synopsis
3. Options with descriptions
4. Examples (most important!)
5. See Also (related commands)

## Template
```
wa <command> - <one-line description>

USAGE:
    wa <command> [OPTIONS] [ARGS]

OPTIONS:
    -o, --option <VALUE>  Description [default: X]

EXAMPLES:
    wa <command> arg              Basic usage
    wa <command> --opt val arg    With option
    wa <command> --dry-run arg    Preview mode

SEE ALSO:
    wa related-cmd    Related functionality
    wa another        Alternative approach
```

## Implementation
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(
    about = "Send text to a pane",
    long_about = None,
    after_help = SEND_EXAMPLES,
)]
struct SendCmd { ... }

const SEND_EXAMPLES: &str = r#"
EXAMPLES:
    wa send --pane 3 "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --wait-for ">>>" "continue"

SEE ALSO:
    wa status       List available panes
    wa why          Explain send failures
"#;
```

## Validation
CI check ensures:
- All commands have help text
- All commands have examples
- No broken cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands follow consistent help template
- [ ] All commands have at least 2 examples
- [ ] See Also references are accurate
- [ ] CI validates help text completeness

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

 {"id":"wa-rnf.5","title":"CLI polish tests: completions, aliases, verbosity, help validation","description":"# CLI polish tests (completions, aliases, verbosity, help)

## Goal
Ensure CLI polish features work correctly and consistently, and remain regression-resistant as new commands are added.

## Test categories

### 1) Completion tests
- Static completions generated for all shells.
- Dynamic completions return correct pane IDs and workflow names.
- No duplicate or missing completions.

### 2) Alias tests
- Built-in aliases expand correctly.
- User aliases load from config.
- Alias conflicts detected.
- `wa aliases` output is accurate.

### 3) Verbosity + format tests
- Default output matches expected format.
- `-v` adds expected additional info.
- `-vv` adds debug-level info.
- `--format json` produces valid, schema-stable JSON.

### 4) Help validation
- All commands have help text.
- All commands have examples.
- Cross-references point to real commands.
- No obvious typos in help text.

### 5) Output consistency
- No ANSI when stdout is not a TTY.
- Exit codes are correct (0 success, 1 error).
- Error messages include remediation.
- JSON envelope is consistent across commands.

## Snapshot/contract tests
- Help text snapshots for regression (normalized).
- Status output snapshots at each verbosity level (normalized; stable ordering).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Completion tests for all shells.
- [ ] Alias expansion tests.
- [ ] Verbosity/format tests.
- [ ] Help text validation in CI.
- [ ] Output consistency verified.

## Testing
- Unit: completion generation + alias expansion + verbosity routing.
- Integration: help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- Schema: JSON output validation for `--format json`.
- CI: smoke tests ensure completions and help remain in sync.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:45.496125952Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:06:29.696127224Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-01x","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-rnf.5","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-smm","title":"Workflow: handle_gemini_quota (Gemini API quota management)","description":"# Workflow: handle_gemini_quota

## Purpose
Handle Gemini-specific quota and rate limit scenarios:
- Daily API quota limits
- Requests-per-minute rate limits
- Model-specific token limits

## Detection Patterns
```
# Quota exhausted
"Resource has been exhausted"
"quota exceeded"
"RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED"

# Rate limit
"Too many requests"
"Please slow down"
"429 Too Many Requests"

# Token limit
"maximum token limit"
"context length exceeded"
```

## Workflow Steps

### Step 1: Detect and classify
- Parse error code/message
- Determine: quota vs rate vs token limit
- Extract reset time if available

### Step 2: Quota exhaustion handling
1. Check if alternate API key available
2. If yes: rotate to new key
3. If no: notify user with reset time
4. Persist state for resume at reset

### Step 3: Rate limit handling
1. Extract retry-after header/message
2. Implement exponential backoff
3. Resume when safe

### Step 4: Token limit handling
1. Similar to Claude Code context handling
2. Suggest conversation restart
3. Save state for continuity

## Safety Constraints
- Don't spam retries
- Respect API terms of service
- Preserve conversation state

## Configuration
```toml
[workflows.handle_gemini_quota]
api_key_rotation = false  # Require explicit enable
max_retries = 3
backoff_base_seconds = 60
```

## Testing
- Fixture: quota error patterns
- Fixture: rate limit error patterns
- Unit: backoff calculation
- E2E: quota → notification flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Gemini quota types detected
- [ ] Rate limits handled with backoff
- [ ] API key rotation works when enabled
- [ ] User notified with reset times","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:38.597646095Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:51:26.910509302Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-workflows","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-smm","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-smm","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-smm","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-smm","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-spi","title":"Watch-and-notify mode: wa watch --notify-only for alert-only monitoring","description":"# Watch-and-Notify Mode: Continuous monitoring with alert delivery

## Purpose
Enable wa to run in notification-only mode, alerting on events without auto-handling.

## Use Case
Some users want to be notified of events but handle them manually:
- Learning the system behavior
- High-stakes environments requiring human review
- Debugging automation issues

## Implementation

### CLI Command
```bash
# Watch and notify on all events
$ wa watch --notify-only

# Watch and notify only on specific events
$ wa watch --notify-only --notify-filter "usage_limit,compaction"

# Watch with specific notification channels
$ wa watch --notify-only --notify-via webhook,desktop
```

### Configuration
```toml
[notifications]
# Enable watch-and-notify mode
notify_only = true

# Which events trigger notifications
notify_filter = ["*.usage_limit*", "*.compaction", "*.error"]

# Notification channels
channels = ["webhook", "desktop"]

# Throttling
min_interval_seconds = 60
max_per_hour = 30
```

### NotifyOnlyMode Implementation
```rust
pub struct NotifyOnlyWatcher {
    event_bus: EventBus,
    notifier: Notifier,
    filter: EventFilter,
    throttle: ThrottleState,
}

impl NotifyOnlyWatcher {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        loop {
            let event = self.event_bus.recv().await?;

            if !self.filter.matches(&event) {
                continue;
            }

            if !self.throttle.allow(&event) {
                tracing::debug!("Throttled notification for {:?}", event.rule_id);
                continue;
            }

            // Log event without handling
            tracing::info!(
                event_id = %event.id,
                rule_id = %event.rule_id,
                pane_id = %event.pane_id,
                "Event detected (notify-only mode)"
            );

            // Send notification
            self.notifier.send(Notification {
                title: format!("wa: {}", event.rule_id),
                body: event.to_notification_body(),
                severity: event.severity(),
                actions: event.suggested_actions(),
            }).await?;
        }
    }
}
```

### Notification Body Generation
```rust
impl Event {
    pub fn to_notification_body(&self) -> String {
        format!(
            "Detected: {}\nPane: {} ({})\nTime: {}\n\nSuggested action:\n  {}",
            self.rule_id,
            self.pane_id,
            self.pane_title,
            self.timestamp.format("%H:%M:%S"),
            self.suggested_action().unwrap_or("No action suggested"),
        )
    }
}
```

### Desktop Notification Example
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔔 wa: codex.usage_limit_reached    │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Pane 9 (codex @ /project)           │
│ Detected: 14:30:15                  │
│                                     │
│ Run: wa workflow run handle_usage.. │
│                                     │
│ [Dismiss]  [Handle]  [View Details] │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Testing
- Notify-only mode does not auto-handle events
- Notifications delivered to configured channels
- Throttling works correctly
- Filter patterns match expected events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wa watch --notify-only implemented
- [ ] Event filtering via --notify-filter
- [ ] Throttling prevents notification spam
- [ ] Desktop and webhook channels supported
- [ ] Notification includes suggested actions
